senses.definition: water that is safe to drink or use for food preparation, meeting government health standards for potability
the white liquid we get from cows, sheep, or goats that we drink and use for making cheese, butter, etc.

examples.x: I poured a glass of cold milk to accompany my freshly baked chocolate chip cookies.
Milk is a good source of calcium, which helps in building strong bones and teeth.
She added a splash of milk to her coffee for a creamy taste.
The creamy pasta sauce was made with a combination of milk and grated cheese.

senses.definition: the liquid inside fruits and vegetables or the drink that we make from them

examples.x: They mixed the juices of different fruits to create a delicious and colorful blend.

senses.definition: a thick smooth drink made with crushed fruit, ice cream, yogurt, or milk

examples.x: The café offers a variety of refreshing smoothies, including mango-banana and strawberry-kiwi.
Jack enjoys blending up a protein-packed smoothie after his workout at the gym.
The tropical smoothie, made with pineapple, coconut milk, and mango, is a popular choice on hot summer days.
She likes experimenting with different ingredients to create unique smoothie recipes, such as avocado-blueberry and kale-pineapple.

senses.definition: a cold and non-alcoholic drink that is usually carbonated

examples.x: She grabbed a can of her favorite soft drink from the fridge to cool off on a hot summer day.
He ordered a large soft drink to accompany his meal at the fast-food restaurant.
The convenience store offered a variety of soft drinks, including cola, lemon-lime, and orange soda.
They enjoyed mixing different flavors of soft drinks at the soda fountain.
He liked to sip on a soft drink while watching movies at home, finding it a comforting treat.

senses.definition: any alcoholic beverage, especially those with a high alcohol content considered intoxicating
an alcoholic beverage that contains ethanol, which is a type of alcohol that can cause intoxication when consumed in excessive amounts
(of drinks) without any alcohol

examples.x: She ordered a nonalcoholic cocktail, also known as a mocktail, made with fruit juices and soda water.
He enjoyed a refreshing glass of nonalcoholic beer while socializing with friends at the bar.
The restaurant offered a selection of nonalcoholic beverages, including iced tea and lemonade.
They served nonalcoholic punch at the party for guests who preferred not to drink alcohol.
The grocery store had a section dedicated to nonalcoholic wines and spirits for those abstaining from alcohol.

senses.definition: referring to a substance, typically a beverage or food, that contains caffeine
made primarily from grains such as wheat, rice, corn, or oats
an alcoholic drink made by mixing several drinks together

examples.x: She enjoyed sipping a classic cocktail like a martini or a mojito on special occasions.
The bartender crafted a signature cocktail for the menu, featuring local spirits and fresh herbs.
They celebrated the end of the week with cocktails at their favorite rooftop bar.
She preferred fruity cocktails with tropical flavors, like a piña colada or a mai tai.
He ordered a fruity cocktail with rum, pineapple juice, and grenadine at the bar.

senses.definition: a non-alcoholic beverage made by mixing various juices, sodas, or other non-alcoholic ingredients to create a cocktail-like drink without the addition of alcohol
a drink containing a lot of sugar, caffeine, or other substances that makes one more active

examples.x: After a long night of studying, he reached for an energy drink to help him stay awake.
The athlete drank an energy drink before the race to boost his performance.
Energy drinks often contain caffeine and other stimulants to provide a quick energy boost.
She prefers to drink an energy drink in the morning instead of coffee to kickstart her day.
The athlete avoided energy drinks before the competition.

senses.definition: water which is carbonated or fizzy

examples.x: She ordered a glass of sparkling water with lemon at the restaurant.
He prefers sparkling water over still water because of the bubbles.
The sparkling water added a refreshing fizz to the cocktail.
She mixed juice with sparkling water to make a light and fizzy drink.
Drinking sparkling water after a meal can aid digestion for some people.

senses.definition: a carbonated soft drink that is flavored with quinine and sweetened with sugar or another sweetener
water that has been boiled, collected as vapor, and then condensed back into a liquid to remove impurities and dissolved substances
an alcoholic drink made from crushed apples

examples.x: She enjoyed a chilled glass of apple cider on a crisp autumn day.
They visited a local orchard to pick apples and make their own homemade cider.
The cider had a refreshing taste with hints of cinnamon and clove.

senses.definition: a drink that is alcoholic and made from different types of grain

examples.x: They tasted different types of beer during the brewery tour.
The fridge was stocked with cans of beer for the party.
During the barbecue, everyone enjoyed a cold and refreshing beer.
The Oktoberfest celebration featured traditional German beers, delighting the attendees.

senses.definition: a drink that is alcoholic and mostly made from grape juice

examples.x: The winery produced a variety of wines, including a rich merlot.
The friends gathered for a picnic, bringing along a chilled bottle of rosé wine.

senses.definition: an alcoholic beverage that is typically higher in alcohol content than regular beer or wine
a liquid that has been infused with carbon dioxide gas, creating bubbles and giving it a fizzy texture and taste
an alcoholic drink consumed before going to bed to help one relax and sleep better
a drink which is fizzy, sweet, and contains alcohol
an alcoholic beverage, especially the type containing high amounts of alcohol

examples.x: They enjoyed a few glasses of booze at the bar after work.
He decided to quit drinking booze to improve his health.
She stocked up on booze for the party, buying a variety of wines and spirits.
The police officer confiscated the underage teenager's stash of booze at the party.
The store specialized in imported and craft booze, catering to enthusiasts and collectors.

senses.definition: a beverage consumed right after a different one, often used to help with the taste of a strong or unpleasant drink
alcohol or an alcoholic beverage, commonly consumed in social gatherings

examples.x: They decided to meet for drinks after work to celebrate their colleague's promotion.
The sommelier recommended a bold red wine to pair with their steak dinner, ensuring a perfect drink to complement the meal.
They stopped at a bar to grab a quick drink before continuing their sightseeing tour.

senses.definition: a colloquial term used to describe strong, high-alcohol content alcoholic beverages that can cause a burning sensation when consumed
a drink served with a lot of small pieces of ice

examples.x: She ordered a chocolate frappe to enjoy as a refreshing treat on a hot day.
The café's menu includes a range of fruit-flavored frappes, perfect for summer.
He prefers his frappe blended until smooth with just a hint of sweetness.
The barista topped the caramel frappe with whipped cream and caramel drizzle.
They offer a decaf option for those who enjoy frappes without the caffeine buzz.

senses.definition: a term used to describe homemade or illegally distilled alcohol
the act of pouring out a liquid as an offering to a deity or spirit, or any type of beverage that is consumed in celebration or as part of a ritual
a slang term used to describe cheap, low-quality alcohol that is often high in alcohol content and can be dangerous to consume in large quantities
a type of alcoholic drink typically consumed in the early evening, often while watching the sunset
a small amount of alcoholic beverage or a place where alcoholic drinks are served or consumed, such as a bar or a pub
a non-alcoholic version of a cocktail or mixed drink, often called a "virgin cocktail" or "virgin drink"
a type of beverage made by combining two or more ingredients, often including alcohol
a medicine or drink made by soaking different types of herbs in boiling water
a drink made with dried milk, malt, water and sometimes sugar or chocolate or ice cream, served hot or cold
a cold smooth drink made by mixing milk and ice-cream with fruits, chocolate, etc. as flavor

examples.x: She treated herself to a chocolate milkshake as a special dessert after dinner.
He ordered a strawberry milkshake to go along with his burger and fries at the diner.
They enjoyed sharing a large milkshake with two straws on a hot summer day.
She liked to make her milkshakes at home, experimenting with different flavor combinations.
He craved a milkshake as a nostalgic treat from his childhood, reminding him of carefree days at the soda fountain.

senses.definition: water from underground that contains minerals and gasses, usually bottled and sold

examples.x: She preferred to drink mineral water for its natural purity and mineral content.
He ordered a bottle of sparkling mineral water to accompany his meal at the restaurant.
The grocery store offered a variety of brands of mineral water, ranging from still to sparkling.
They enjoyed a picnic by the lake, sipping on cool bottles of mineral water.
She added a slice of lemon to her mineral water for a hint of citrus flavor.

senses.definition: a drink that is high in protein and is similar to milkshake, consumed to increase energy level, lose weight, or gain muscle
(of tea or coffee) served without any added milk, cream, or sweeteners

examples.x: I prefer my coffee black, so I can enjoy its pure flavor.
For a stronger taste, try the black version of our new herbal blend.

senses.definition: a version that is modified to have lower calories, sugar, fat, or other specific nutritional components for health or weight management purposes
(of wine) contaminated by a faulty cork, resulting in an unpleasant odor or taste in the wine
(of tea or coffee) having had caffeine completely or partly removed

examples.x: The café offers both regular and decaffeinated options for their drinks.
The label clearly states whether the coffee is regular or decaffeinated.

senses.definition: (of a drink) suitable or safe for consuming

examples.x: The water from the tap is drinkable and meets safety standards.
After boiling, the river water became drinkable for the hikers.
The label on the bottle indicates that the contents are drinkable and free from contaminants.
The filtered water dispenser provides cold and refreshing drinkable water.
The homemade lemonade is freshly prepared and perfectly drinkable on a hot summer day.

senses.definition: (of alcohol, especially wine) having little to no sweetness

examples.x: He prefers a dry red wine that complements the richness of the meal.
The dry champagne had crisp flavors, making it a perfect choice for celebrations.
Many wine enthusiasts appreciate the complexity of dry whites over sweeter varieties.
The sommelier recommended a dry rosé that pairs well with seafood.
After tasting several options, she decided on a dry gin for her cocktail.

senses.definition: a type of drink that produces bubbles or fizz, often through the addition of carbon dioxide, creating a refreshing and invigorating texture and taste

examples.x: As the seltzer bottle was shaken, its contents became vigorously effervescent with rapid bubble formation.

senses.definition: (of a fizzy drink) not having bubbles anymore

examples.x: I accidentally left my soda out, and now it's flat.
He took a sip of his flat cola and grimaced.
The bottle had been open for hours, so the lemonade was completely flat.
She prefers her sparkling water flat rather than fizzy.
After sitting out all afternoon, the beer was totally flat.

senses.definition: refers to a type of drink that contains high levels of alcohol and can cause impairment or intoxication if consumed in excess
describing drink that contains extra salt and minerals to replace the ones a person loses during exercising or playing a sport
(particularly of alcoholic drinks) not mixed with anything
(of drinks, especially alcoholic drinks) with no other substance mixed into it
a hot or cold beverage made from cocoa powder, milk or water, and sweetener, such as sugar

examples.x: Sarah likes to make a steaming cup of hot cocoa on cold winter nights, topped with marshmallows.
He liked to add a splash of peppermint extract to his cocoa for a festive holiday twist.
The café offered a variety of cocoa options, including classic, dark chocolate, and white chocolate.
They enjoyed sipping cold cocoa on a warm summer day, finding it refreshing and indulgent.
She prepared a steaming cup of cocoa on a snowy afternoon, savoring its comforting warmth.

senses.definition: (of tea or coffee) served with milk or cream

examples.x: The café specializes in white beverages for those who like a creamier taste.
Her morning routine always includes a white cup of tea with a slice of toast.

senses.definition: (of a drink) not having bubbles in it
a type of multi-layered drink made by carefully layering different colored liqueurs or cordials in a narrow, straight-sided glass
a type of beverage that has been made by fermenting grains or other ingredients
